subject: Mathematics
coverageNote: Form directories exist only where teaching-module source PDFs were ingested. Missing forms must resolve as known-gap, not empty objects.

lesson.title: POLYGONS
topics: Polygons surround us in ways both obvious and subtle. They are two-dimensional shapes that form surfaces of
three-dimensional shapes such as pavers, road signs, windows and in the intricate geometric patterns woven
into traditional fabrics and crafts passed down through generations. These shapes formed by straight lines
meeting at angles are more than mere drawings on paper; they represent order, symmetry, and structure in the
activities: Cut outs of polygons (both regular and irregular)
